notes_md:
**được cái** = *at least / on the bright side*. It highlights one good point to balance something not-so-good.

It’s a linking phrase, usually after a negative or weak point, to add a positive contrast. The tone is slightly defensive or trying to be fair.


Close in feeling to **ít ra** (at least), but *được cái* sounds more like “well, the good thing is…”.
exampleSentencesTranslated: He is not good at studying, at least he is very hardworking.
She does not cook very well, at least she is always cheerful.
I do not have much experience, at least I learn very quickly.
notes:
được cái = at least / on the bright side. It highlights one good point to balance something not-so-good.

It’s a linking phrase, usually after a negative or weak point, to add a positive contrast. The tone is slightly defensive or trying to be fair.

Close in feeling to ít ra (at least), but được cái sounds more like “well, the good thing is…”.
exampleSentences: Anh ấy không học giỏi nhưng được cái rất chăm chỉ.
Chị nấu ăn không ngon lắm, được cái chị luôn vui vẻ.
Em chưa có nhiều kinh nghiệm, được cái em học rất nhanh.
